Author: deempak
Content: This could be a digital art 😄.

Author: tholin
Content:
- rv32ima_zicsr core in 6502-compatible pinout
- 6502/6510 clone (cycle-accurate, now hopefully with the bus timing fixed)
- C64 PLA
- Nanoprocessor clone, 8-bit educational CPU, LED blinker
- The two [FeatherLane](https://codeberg.org/TholinVali/FeatherLane) -generated layouts: rv32i core and a VERY simple CPU that is a 32-bit version of [MCPU](https://github.com/cpldcpu/MCPU/tree/master) 
- C64 SID clone
- C64 SID clone, but I added FM synthesis features on top (fully backwards-compatible)
- General-purpose IO peripheral block you can hook up to an existing CPU
- Test NTSC signal generator (now in color - I have it set up displaying some pixel art or pride flags)
- The R2R DACs (hopefully the output stage isn’t power hungry anymore)
- Another copy of 32-bit MCPU, except its using Dynamic DFFs (we’ll see how that works out on this process node)
- The Palette, which contains samples of everything I can draw and experiments with metal spacing - will give microscope images of this to any artists that agree to create silicon art for me in the future, to give them more options for "colors"

Author: tholin
Content: The Dynamic DFF MCPU hits an fmax of 125MHz on the typical corner. The Dynamic DFFs would be such an upgrade, if they end up working.
